description: Relationship-type semantics for intellectual genealogy classification.

values.name: Direct Precursor
values.use_when: the prior work transmits the target's immediate problem-space, unresolved aporia, or architecturally central precursor problem.
values.avoid_when: the prior work mainly contributes method, reusable vocabulary, or same-program continuity without being the target's forcing problem.
values.secondary_when: the work also contributes methodology or provides a foil.

values.name: Methodological Ancestor
values.use_when: the prior work mainly contributes procedure, style of critique, analytical method, or reusable conceptual apparatus rather than the target's central problem.
values.avoid_when: the prose clearly identifies transmitted aporia/problem-space as the main inheritance.
values.secondary_when: the work also acts as a precursor in a narrower argumentative sense.

values.name: Counter Position
values.use_when: the target develops by turning the prior work into an explicit foil, inversion, or object of immanent self-critique.
values.avoid_when: the prior work is mainly supportive or upstream contextual background.
values.secondary_when: the target still inherits vocabulary or method from the criticized work.

values.name: Indirect Contextualizer
values.use_when: the prior work mainly frames the background conditions or horizon within which the target becomes intelligible.
values.avoid_when: the prior work directly shapes the target's architecture, method, or key concepts.

values.name: Stylistic Influence
values.use_when: the inheritance is primarily rhetorical, tonal, or compositional rather than conceptual or methodological.
values.avoid_when: the relationship is structurally analytical or problem-transmitting.

values.name: Conceptual Sibling
values.use_when: the prior work belongs to the same authorial project, formation, or research program and shares vocabulary/frameworks, but is better understood as an adjacent developmental installment than as the target's immediate forcing problem.
values.avoid_when: the prose identifies a specific transmitted aporia/problem-space as the dominant relation.
values.secondary_when: the work also shares method or limited precursor elements.

guidance: Do not default multiple important works to `direct_precursor`.
Same-author earlier works are not automatically `direct_precursor`.
Keep the primary label maximally discriminating; use the secondary label for the next-most-important role.

problem_transmission_signals: problem it was written to solve
method_only_markers: new domain absent from the prior work
same_program_markers: within a single developing body of thought
